title: eriogonum jamesii subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum jamesii subsp. typicum, also known as James' buckwheat, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum jamesii subsp. typicum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for internal use, and its root and leaves can be dried and used in smoking blends.
